Risk 4 (LOW): No Intelligence Core files are modified; runtime behaviour cannot change. All guardrail code is test/validation-only.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:297-310 — 10 STOP conditions: (1) required authority files missing — pre-verified clear; (2) Sentinel structure cannot be located — pre-verified clear; (3) guardrails require broad runtime refactoring — must not trigger; (4) validator cannot detect key drift programmatically — must not trigger; (5) any launch-active authority remains ambiguous — pre-verified clear; (6) tests would need to rely only on documentation — not acceptable; (7) raw investigation spec runtime reads found and cannot be resolved — pre-verified clear; (8) frontend inference patterns found and cannot be safely resolved — must verify; (9) package provenance classification incomplete — pre-verified complete (186/186 from ARCH-RT-5D); (10) guard produces unstable false positives without better path — must not trigger.

The prompt prescribes 'sentinel/packs/day_one_architecture_guardrails.yaml' (YAML format). However: (1) All 4 existing sentinel packs are .json: escaped_defects_v1.json, lc_s8d_unit_governance_v1.json, lc_s10b_launch_core_protection_v1.json, scaffold_lc_s20_22_replay_render_v1.json. (2) sentinel/sentinel_runner.py:207 — pack loading uses json.load() only: 'with open(ESCAPED_DEFECTS_PACK, encoding="utf-8") as f: return json.load(f)'. No YAML loading exists in the runner. (3) Creating a .yaml file would be non-functional — the runner cannot load it and the established convention is .json. CURSOR MUST: Create sentinel/packs/day_one_architecture_guardrails_v1.json (JSON format, matching convention of escaped_defects_v1.json). Do not create a .yaml file.

sentinel/sentinel_runner.py:47-69 — DEFECT_CLASS_TESTS is a hardcoded dict in the runner source, not loaded from the pack JSON files. The pack JSON files are reference documents; actual test routing for sentinel_runner.py --defect-class runs requires entries in DEFECT_CLASS_TESTS. Modifying sentinel_runner.py is a HIGH-risk control-plane action (per SOP and CLAUDE.md §7 and §13). Cursor must choose one of: (a) Create a reference-only JSON pack documenting the guardrail defect classes but do NOT modify sentinel_runner.py — document that runner integration is a follow-on task; (b) If modifying sentinel_runner.py, classify this as HIGH-risk boundary-file modification and document it explicitly in the sprint closure report. Option (a) is the safe path that does not require control-plane modification. The sprint prompt says the Sentinel pack should allow 'Sentinel to fail before merge' — this is achievable via the architecture guardrail pytest tests directly, without sentinel_runner.py modification.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:237 — 'The script must fail non-zero on guardrail violation. It must not mutate files.' The validator must use sys.exit(1) or equivalent on any violation. It must also be runnable standalone: 'python backend/scripts/validate_day_one_architecture.py'. Cursor must verify the validator's main() follows this contract and test it. The guardrail rules 1-26 from the prompt must map to explicit validator checks — not just print statements.
